text: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, you want sweetness to be kept in check.
15.2g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for fruit, the real question is whether the sweetness stays under control.
0.4g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. for a fruit, sugar moderation is the whole game.
the rest is not doing much.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

the upsides first: usually beats ultra-processed sweet snacks. that replacement value matters in real life. easy to use as a whole food. simple, recognisable, and realistic for everyday eating. ingredient simplicity is hard to argue with. whole-fruit clarity is part of the category appeal.

on the weaker side: easy to eat quickly when it is very sweet. that can blur the line between light and sugar-heavy portions. satiety is not especially strong. fruit often needs help from the rest of the meal. protein contribution is tiny. that is normal for fruit, but it still limits nutritional breadth.
B tier. Best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
